channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Soil may be a source of resistance to antibiotics, new research suggests
item.description: New research suggests drought can stoke antibiotic resistance in soil bacteria, and those genes can end up in human pathogens.
item.content:encoded: New research suggests drought can stoke antibiotic resistance in soil bacteria, and those genes can end up in human pathogens.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: The Major League Baseball season officially begins
item.description: Major League Baseball gets underway Wednesday when the San Francisco Giants host the New York Yankees. The LA Dodgers are favorites to repeat as champions, and a labor battle could be on the horizon.
item.content:encoded: Major League Baseball gets underway Wednesday when the San Francisco Giants host the New York Yankees. The LA Dodgers are favorites to repeat as champions, and a labor battle could be on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: The 82nd Airborne is headed to the Middle East. Do we know why?
item.description: Thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division are expected to deploy to the Middle East. Iran publicly rejected a ceasefire proposal, though the White House says talks continue.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division are expected to deploy to the Middle East. Iran publicly rejected a ceasefire proposal, though the White House says talks continue.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: The secret campaign within the Pentagon to bring AI into combat
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Bloomberg reporter Katrina Manson about her new book, Project Maven, and the secret campaign within the Pentagon to bring AI into combat.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Bloomberg reporter Katrina Manson about her new book, Project Maven, and the secret campaign within the Pentagon to bring AI into combat.
item.dc:creator: Elena Burnett

item.title: National Mall is propaganda battlefield for Trump and his critics
item.description: Banners bearing the face of President Trump vie with satiric statues and protest posters in a propaganda battle playing out in and around the National Mall.
item.content:encoded: Banners bearing the face of President Trump vie with satiric statues and protest posters in a propaganda battle playing out in and around the National Mall.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Hindu minority in Bangladesh faces surge in attacks after 2024 uprising
item.description: Ever since mass protests ousted Bangladesh's leader Sheikh Hasina in August 2024, minorities have faced a surge of vigilante attacks.
item.content:encoded: Ever since mass protests ousted Bangladesh's leader Sheikh Hasina in August 2024, minorities have faced a surge of vigilante attacks.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: What the future of the U.S. war in Iran might look like for Gulf Arab nations
item.description: The Arab countries of the Gulf opposed the U.S. war in Iran. Yet they face some of the heaviest attacks from Iran and feel the war could leave them less secure than when the fighting began.
item.content:encoded: The Arab countries of the Gulf opposed the U.S. war in Iran. Yet they face some of the heaviest attacks from Iran and feel the war could leave them less secure than when the fighting began.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zamora

item.title: OpenAI's Sora app may be going away, but its legacy will be the spread of AI video slop
item.description: Barely six months after its launch, OpenAI is ending an app that could generate AI video at the click of a button.
item.content:encoded: Barely six months after its launch, OpenAI is ending an app that could generate AI video at the click of a button.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel
